bucket_summary.diagnosis: v4.7 treats benign auth/login vocabulary as enough evidence for brute force.
bucket_summary.next_action: Keep these as paired contrasts; brute force must require repeated attempts, a rate/window, or blocking evidence.
bucket_summary.title: Normal auth negative

bucket_summary.diagnosis: v4.7 underweights SQL payload syntax when the route looks like login or account activity.
bucket_summary.next_action: Make SQL payload tokens in username, email, or query fields own the label over generic auth route context.
bucket_summary.title: SQLi inside auth context

bucket_summary.diagnosis: v4.7 keeps brute-force label recall, but escalates all medium examples to high.
bucket_summary.next_action: Add a medium severity contract: high requires larger volume, spread, privileged target, lockout, or confirmed blocking impact.
bucket_summary.title: Brute force medium severity

bucket_summary.diagnosis: v4.7 mostly keeps the recon label, but one medium/high boundary remains unstable.
bucket_summary.next_action: Keep only a small severity pair for recon; do not broaden the recon taxonomy.
bucket_summary.title: Port/recon severity boundary

bucket_summary.diagnosis: v4.7 passes the single traversal guard in this probe.
bucket_summary.next_action: Keep this as a regression guard rather than a main v4.8 data target.
bucket_summary.title: Directory traversal guard

v4_6_on_v4_7_probe.note: Endpoint inventory on 2026-05-23 did not expose the v4.6 alias, so the audit uses heuristic and base Qwen3.5 comparators first.

decision.summary: v4.7 stays held. The next action is a small v4.8 paired-contrast repair after this diagnostic audit, not a broad dataset expansion or fixed-split run.
